title: Standing-privilege credential vaulting drift — admin accounts outside PAM
exclusions: Does NOT evaluate usage of credentials (covered by 22.40.1); focuses on credentials sitting outside the vault entirely.
evidence: Saved search 'pam_vaulting_drift' (daily) cross-references IdP privileged groups against PAM vault inventory and reports accounts not present in the vault.

compliance.assurance_rationale: AC-5 separation of duties and privileged-account vaulting are directly enforced.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST-800-53 AC-5 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.40.4: Standing-privilege credential vaulting drift — admin accounts outside PAM.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.40.4' running on IdP privileged-group membership (Azure AD, Okta, LDAP), PAM vault inventory (CyberArk, BeyondTrust, Delinea)., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 7.2.5.1 requires tight controls on privileged account lifecycle; unvaulted admins fail this control.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that PCI-DSS 7.2.5.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.40.4: Standing-privilege credential vaulting drift — admin accounts outside PAM.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.40.4' running on IdP privileged-group membership (Azure AD, Okta, LDAP), PAM vault inventory (CyberArk, BeyondTrust, Delinea)., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: Unvaulted privileged credentials are a textbook SOX-ITGC material weakness.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that SOX-ITGC ITGC.AccessMgmt.Privileged (Privileged access) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.40.4: Standing-privilege credential vaulting drift — admin accounts outside PAM.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.40.4' running on IdP privileged-group membership (Azure AD, Okta, LDAP), PAM vault inventory (CyberArk, BeyondTrust, Delinea)., archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A domain-admin account exists in AzureAD but has no matching record in the CyberArk vault; the UC fires the next day.
controlTest.negativeScenario: All IdP admin accounts are present in the vault; no alert.

dataSources: IdP privileged-group membership (Azure AD, Okta, LDAP), PAM vault inventory (CyberArk, BeyondTrust, Delinea).
spl:
| rest /services/identity/privileged-groups splunk_server=*
| fields user group
| join type=outer user [| rest /services/pam/vault-inventory splunk_server=* | fields user vault_id]
| where isnull(vault_id)
| stats values(group) as groups by user
| table user groups
description: Treats the PAM vault as the authoritative register of privileged credentials and alerts on any IdP-level admin that is not present in the vault. The asymmetry between 'privileged' and 'managed' is the most common drift pattern.
value: Replaces the quarterly 'access-review export + spreadsheet join' with a daily evidence feed.
implementation: (1) Expose IdP privileged groups and PAM vault inventory via REST; (2) run daily; (3) on miss, auto-open a PAM onboarding ticket with the CISO.
visualization: Table of drift accounts by group, bar chart of drift count by tenant, single-value '% of admins vaulted'. Key fields: `groups`, `user`.

references.title: MITRE ATT&CK — T1078.003 Valid Accounts: Local Accounts

knownFalsePositives: Privileged access logs during planned change windows, scheduled audits, or vendor-supervised maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: IdP privileged-group membership (Azure AD, Okta, LDAP), PAM vault inventory (CyberArk, BeyondTrust, Delinea)..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Expose IdP privileged groups and PAM vault inventory via REST; (2) run daily; (3) on miss, auto-open a PAM onboarding ticket with the CISO.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
| rest /services/identity/privileged-groups splunk_server=*
| fields user group
| join type=outer user [| rest /services/pam/vault-inventory splunk_server=* | fields user vault_id]
| where isnull(vault_id)
| stats values(group) as groups by user
| table user groups
```

Understanding this SPL

**Standing-privilege credential vaulting drift — admin accounts outside PAM** — Replaces the quarterly 'access-review export + spreadsheet join' with a daily evidence feed.

Documented **Data sources**: IdP privileged-group membership (Azure AD, Okta, LDAP), PAM vault inventory (CyberArk, BeyondTrust, Delinea). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Calls Splunk `rest` to read configuration or REST-exposed entities.
• Keeps or drops fields with `fields` to shape columns and size.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(vault_id)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` aggregates events by **user**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.

Output fields: groups, user.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare results with the audit register and the GRC system so evidence lines up with control owners, attestations, and retention policy.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table of drift accounts by group, bar chart of drift count by tenant, single-value '% of admins vaulted'.
grandmaExplanation: We help you show who used powerful accounts and when, so access reviews and investigations have a clear story.
